title: Piccle 1.0 document
description: Piccle v1.0 schema — Piccle's declarative format for short procedural audio micro-interactions. See https://dotpiccle.com for the full specification.

$schema.description: Canonical Piccle v1 JSON Schema URI. Optional, but when present it must identify this schema.

piccle.description: The Piccle format version this document targets. '1.0' is the only valid value for v1.

name.description: Optional human-readable name for this sound.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of what this sound is for.

duration_ms.description: Total document duration in milliseconds. Optional — if absent, computed from the latest-ending layer. Shorter trims layers; longer pads with silence. With spatial effects, duration_ms plus each effect's tail extension must remain within the safe-integer bound.

master_volume_level.description: Final master gain applied to the spatial-effects result before clipping. 0 = silent, 1 = unity gain. A single number, unlike layer `volume` which accepts a contour object. Independent of per-layer volume.

spatial_effects.description: Optional whole-document. Effects are applied serially in array order, all starting at the document origin. An empty array is valid and equivalent to omitting the field — no spatial effects are applied.

layers.description: One or more layers that make up this sound (no spec maximum; engines MAY enforce their own). Each layer is an independent sound generator with its own volume, balance, and filter chain.

curves.description: Transition curve shapes used in volume, pitch, and filter frequency entries.

fade_stage.description: A fade stage: a duration plus the curve used to interpolate the fade gain. Used mainly for fade_in and fade_out

ms.description: Fade duration in milliseconds. 0 means no fade stage.

curve.description: Curve applied to the fade gain. Reuses the transition_curve enum.

filter.description: A single filter applied to a layer. Type is fixed for the layer duration. Cutoff frequency uses an always-array describing motion over time.

type.description: Filter type. lowpass keeps lows, highpass keeps highs, bandpass keeps a focused frequency region.

frequencies.description: One or more cutoff-frequency targets evaluated from layer time zero.

hz.description: Cutoff frequency in Hz at this point. 20–20000 Hz range.

hold_ms.description: How long to hold at this cutoff before transitioning to the next, in milliseconds.

transition_ms.description: How long to move from this cutoff to the next, in milliseconds. Ignored on the last entry.

transition_curve.description: Shape of the transition from this cutoff to the next. Ignored on the last entry.

resonance.description: How much the filter resonates at its frequency, 0–1. 0 = flat/no resonance, 1 = strong resonance (Q ≈ 12). Like a struck bell singing at the cutoff frequency.

layer.description: A single layer in a Piccle document. Each layer is one sound generator (tone or noise) with volume contour, stereo position, and an optional filter chain.

id.description: Unique identifier for this layer. Lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only.

start_ms.description: When this layer starts, in milliseconds from the document start. Defaults to 0. Layers that omit start_ms all start at the document start simultaneously; the order of layers in the layers array does not affect timing.

duration_ms.description: How long this layer plays, in milliseconds. start_ms plus duration_ms must remain within the safe-integer bound.

source.description: The raw sound this layer makes. A tone (waveform + pitch) or noise (character).

volume.description: Loudness contour. A number (0–1) for a constant level, or an object with fade_in_ms, fade_out_ms, and a levels array.

balance.description: Stereo position. -1 = full left, 0 = center, 1 = full right.

filters.description: Filter chain applied in series (filter 1 output feeds filter 2). Zero or more filters (engine-defined maximum).

pitch.description: Pitch specification for a tone source. Always has a frequencies array (no static shortcuts). Optional offset_cents for chorus/detuning.

frequencies.description: One or more pitch-frequency targets evaluated from layer time zero.

hz.description: Target pitch in Hz at this point. 20–20000 Hz range.

hold_ms.description: How long to hold at this pitch before transitioning to the next, in milliseconds.

transition_ms.description: How long to move from this pitch to the next, in milliseconds. Ignored on the last entry.

transition_curve.description: Shape of the transition from this pitch to the next. Ignored on the last entry.

offset_cents.description: Pitch offset in cents (100 cents = 1 semitone). Applied after contour interpolation and before render-profile frequency clamping.

spatial_effect.description: A spatial effect applied to the whole document. Each entry has a type discriminator and type-specific fields. Effects are applied serially in array order. Property validation (including additionalProperties: false) is delegated to the type-specific branches spatial_reverb and spatial_echo; the router itself only enforces object-ness and the type enum.

type.description: The spatial effect type. reverb for a diffuse room tail, echo for discrete repeats.

spatial_reverb.description: A reverb effect — gives a one-shot sound a short sense of space. All three reverb fields are required.

type.description: Discriminator identifying this entry as a reverb effect.

amount.description: Dry/wet crossfade. 0 = fully dry, 1 = fully wet (dry removed).

tail_ms.description: Reverb RT60 target and emitted wet-tail duration, in milliseconds.

soften_hz.description: Corner frequency of the normative first-order wet-path lowpass, in Hz.

spatial_echo.description: An echo effect — adds one or more discrete repeats of the dry signal. Use delay_ms to control spacing, feedback to control Repeat count, wet_gain for how loud the echoes are, and damp_hz to progressively darken each repeat. All four fields are required. For simple understandable audio terms: delay_ms = how long between echoes, feedback = how much comes back, wet_gain = how much echo you hear, damp_hz = how muffled each repeat gets.

type.description: Discriminator identifying this entry as an echo effect.

delay_ms.description: Time between successive echoes in milliseconds, and the time to the first echo. 1 or more.

feedback.description: How much of each echo is fed back into the delay line. 0 = a single echo (one repeat); approaching 1 = many repeats, very long tail. Must be strictly less than 1 for stability. The echo tail length is derived from delay_ms and feedback via tail_ms_effective = delay_ms × (1 + ceil(log(0.001)/log(feedback))); high feedback combined with long delay_ms produces very long output. Authors are responsible for choosing values appropriate to their use case.

wet_gain.description: How much of the echo is heard. 0 = no echo (dry only); 1 = echo at full level added on top of the dry signal. Additive — the dry is always present at full level regardless of this value.

damp_hz.description: Corner frequency of the first-order lowpass in the feedback path, in Hz. Higher = brighter repeats (less damping); lower = darker, more muffled repeats (stronger damping). 200–12000 Hz range.

source.description: The raw sound generator for a layer. Either a tone (pitched hum with a waveform) or noise (pitchless hiss with a character). The two variants are structurally distinct so validation is automatic.

type.description: A pitched hum — a sound with a recognizable pitch you could hum or whistle back.

wave.description: The waveform shape. sine = smooth/soft, triangle = warmer, square = buzzy/hollow, saw = bright/buzzy.

pitch.description: The pitch of this tone — always a frequencies array with optional offset_cents.

type.description: A pitchless hiss — a sound with no note, just texture.

character.description: The spectral character of deterministic noise. soft is a 400 Hz first-order lowpass, neutral is unfiltered uniform noise, and sharp is a 2 kHz first-order highpass. See docs/09-noise-and-determinism.md.

seed.description: Unsigned 32-bit seed for the normative PCG32 noise stream. Equal character and seed values reproduce the same raw stream.

volume.description: Loudness contour for a layer. A plain number is shorthand for a constant level with anti-click fade-out. An object allows multi-level contours with per-direction fade curves.

oneOf.description: Shorthand for a constant level at this value with a 5 ms linear fade-out.

fade_in.description: Fade-in from silence to the first declared level. Defaults to no fade-in (0 ms, linear).

fade_out.description: Fade-out from the last declared level to silence. Defaults to a 5 ms linear fade for anti-click. Use curve: "exponential" for natural decay tails.

levels.description: One or more linear-amplitude targets for the layer volume contour.

level.description: Target loudness at this point. Always a plain number.

hold_ms.description: How long to hold at this level before transitioning to the next, in milliseconds.

transition_ms.description: How long to move from this level to the next, in milliseconds. Ignored on the last entry.

transition_curve.description: Shape of the transition from this level to the next. Ignored on the last entry.
